channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: First hostages set for release after Israel-Hamas ceasefire's bumpy start
item.description: Qatar, a key negotiator in the fragile Israel-Hamas ceasefire agreement, confirmed Saturday that the first hostages will be released in less than 24 hours. Qatari Foreign Minister Majid al-Ansari said the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip is set to begin at 8:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Gunman shoots dead 2 judges in Iran's capital tied to 1988 mass executions
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — A man fatally shot two prominent hard-line judges in Iran's capital Saturday, officials said, both of whom allegedly took part in the mass execution of dissidents in 1988.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cease-fire between Israel, Hamas to go into effect Sunday morning, Qatar says
item.description: The cease-fire between Hamas and Israel is expected to go into effect on Sunday at 8:30 a.m. local time, Qatar’s foreign ministry announced Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump vowed to end the war in Ukraine quickly but Moscow and Kyiv are digging in before any talks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has pledged to broker a peace deal in Ukraine, but as he prepares to take office, peace seems as elusive as ever.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Impeached South Korean president argues for his release as court reviews his formal arrest
item.description: South Korea’s impeached president has argued for his release before a Seoul judge as the court reviewed whether to grant a law enforcement request for his formal arrest.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel's Cabinet approves a deal for a ceasefire in Gaza and the release of dozens of hostages
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s Cabinet approved a deal early Saturday for a ceasefire in Gaza that would release dozens of hostages held there and pause the 15-month war with Hamas, bringing the sides a step closer to ending their deadliest and most destructive fighting ever.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, WAFAA SHURAFA and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli government approves ceasefire deal: Reports
item.description: The Israeli government has formally approved the ceasefire deal in Gaza and the release of some hostages, soon halting the conflict that has been raging for 15 months.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Attorneys for fire victim say utility may have destroyed evidence of what caused deadly LA-area fire
item.description: Attorneys for a woman who is among thousands who lost their homes in the Eaton Fire outside Los Angeles say Southern California Edison crews working to repair and restore power in the area may have destroyed evidence that could help determine what sparked the wildfire.
item.dc:creator: Jason Dearen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia and Iran sign a partnership treaty to deepen their ties in the face of Western sanctions
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin and his Iranian counterpart, Masoud Pezeshkian, signed a broad cooperation pact Friday as their countries deepened their partnership in the face of stinging Western sanctions.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Xi discuss TikTok, trade in call before inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump said Friday he spoke with Chinese President Xi Jinping, with the two leaders discussing trade and the fate of the popular video-sharing app TikTok. "The call was a very good one for both China and the U.S.A.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: South African miner describes horrors for those who spent months underground
item.description: STILFONTEIN, South Africa. (AP) — Dozens of men sat and lay together in small caves, some so shallow they couldn’t stand up. The air was wet and stuffy, and wracking coughs echoed in the confined space. Bodies wrapped in fabric and twine were set aside in rows nearby.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds of dead sea turtles are washing ashore on India's coast
item.description: BENGALURU, India (AP) — Over 400 endangered sea turtles have washed ashore on India's east coast near the city of Chennai in the last two weeks in an event not witnessed in over two decades. Olive ridley turtles travel thousands of miles for ideal nesting conditions on India’s coast.
item.dc:creator: SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French president in Lebanon to meet the country's new leaders and discuss ceasefire with Israel
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — France’s president began a visit to Lebanon Friday, where he will meet the crisis-hit country’s newly elected leaders, as the nation attempts to recover from the 14-month Israel-Hezbollah war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More than 40 Pakistanis feared drowned in the capsizing of a migrant boat off African coast
item.description: Officials say more than 40 Pakistanis are feared to have drowned in the capsizing of a migrant boat off West Africa’s Atlantic coastline.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Xi's special representative Han Zheng to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: China’s vice president will attend President-elect Trump’s inauguration, according to a Thursday statement from the Foreign Ministry Spokesperson. “At U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: UK leader Starmer signs ‘100-year partnership’ agreement with Ukraine during trip to Kyiv
item.description: As part of a European show of support, U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer has signed a 100-year partnership agreement with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis injured as Vatican confirms 2nd fall in matter of weeks
item.description: Pope Francis fell for the second time in six weeks on Thursday, injuring his right arm and requiring a sling, the Vatican says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis hurts his right arm after falling for the second time in just over a month
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis fell Thursday and hurt his right arm, the Vatican said, just weeks after another apparent fall resulted in a bad bruise on his chin. Francis didn’t break his arm, but a sling was put on as a precaution, the Vatican spokesman said in a statement On Dec.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu says 'last-minute crisis' with Hamas holding up approval of Gaza truce and hostage deal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office said Thursday his Cabinet won’t meet to approve the agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages until Hamas backs down, accusing the group of reneging on parts of the agreement in an atte
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu delays Gaza cease-fire vote, accusing Hamas of trying to back out of deal
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday accused Hamas of backing out of a cease-fire deal to release hostages and bring a pause to more than a year of fighting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Details need to be addressed in Israel-Hamas ceasefire
item.description: When a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is finalized depends on the handling of specific implementation details
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to lift Cuba terrorism designation, reversing Trump decision in final week in office
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Tuesday that it will remove Cuba from the list of State Sponsors of Terrorism (SST) in exchange for the release of political prisoners jailed for protesting against the regime in July 2021.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: What to make of the final polling of the Biden years
item.description: A January 10-11 survey of 1,000 registered voters found President Joe Biden with a 37% approval rating in the final week of his presidency, 12 points lower than when Emerson first polled his approval in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico sends firefighters to Los Angeles to help battle devastating wildfires
item.description: Disaster relief workers from the National Forestry Commission and the Ministry of Defense in Mexico also arrived to assist local emergency workers.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Significant progress made in talks over Israel-Hamas ceasefire and hostage release, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — U.S. and Arab mediators made significant progress overnight toward brokering a ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war and the release of scores of hostages held in the Gaza Strip, but a deal hasn't been reached yet, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: VICTORIA EASTWOOD, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Baby gorilla finds home at Istanbul zoo after rescue
item.description: Five-month-old Zeytin is recovering at an Istanbul zoo after being rescued from a cargo hold on a Turkish Airlines flight.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Biden takes victory lap in final foreign policy speech
item.description: Biden is expected to discuss wars in Ukraine, Middle East and aggression from China, Iran and North Korea.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Canada deploying 60 firefighters to help with California blazes
item.description: In a post on X, Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Canada was helping aid the Los Angeles area as several wildfires continue devastating the area.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Family of American hostage in Gaza remains hopeful their son, 21, will return
item.description: Edan Alexander, an American hostage, celebrated his second birthday in captivity after Hamas kidnapped him more than a year ago.
item.dc:creator: Hena Doba, NewsNation
